mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp increase in the unemployment rate over 2 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in housing starts over 3 months signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in industrial production over 2 months signals a strengthening supply environment that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 30% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1.5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate over 2 months signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in real GDP over 2 quarters signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp decrease in the unemployment rate over 2 months signals a strengthening labor market that increases aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the federal funds rate is below 1.5%.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 30% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1.5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in retail sales over 3 months signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only when the industrial production is above 95.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
